summary: Tested Driver mobile-web app end-to-end. Backend: 11/11 pytest cases pass (admin driver create returns login_password + login_username; phone+password login; bad password→401; regenerate-password rotates and old pwd is rejected; /auth/me shows role=Driver+driver_id; /driver/schedules empty list; tracking settings interval; PUT /tenants/current/settings persists gps_ping_interval_minutes; ping rejected w/o valid in_progress schedule; delete cascades to user). Frontend: /driver/login renders mobile-only; admin /admin/drivers Add Driver opens dialog, on submit 'Share these credentials' modal displays mobile+system password with Copy/Done buttons; driver successfully logs in via UI and lands on /driver/schedules with empty-state card and minimal layout (no DashboardLayout). LiveDriverMap not visually verified end-to-end (would require a real distributor login + an in_progress schedule + a posted ping); backend tracking endpoint authorisation is unit-tested via pytest.

issues: Driver name and phone are visually concatenated in DOM text (no separator between full_name and phone) — render is fine but copy/select shows 'TEST UI Drv 0434227704342288'; consider a space/comma between them.

action_items: (Optional polish) Add aria/data-testid attributes on the Drivers create form inputs (full_name, phone, license_number, city) and on the credentials modal Copy button to improve testability.
(Optional) Add visible separator between driver name and phone in the /driver/schedules header.

End-to-end verification of LiveDriverMap polling on Distributor schedule detail still pending — was not exercised because no in_progress schedule with pings existed; the backend tracking endpoint is covered by pytest.
critical_code_review_comments: driver_app.py /login sets cookie with secure=True+samesite=none — fine on prod HTTPS but won't be set on plain HTTP (e.g. local dev).
GPSPing.recorded_at is accepted as a free-form string with no ISO validation.
list_driver_schedules default window is today..today+1 (Asia tz vs UTC) — drivers in IST may miss late-night schedules around midnight; consider tenant timezone.
complete_stop returns early when delivery already delivered but does not re-evaluate auto-completion — minor edge case.
admin_drivers _provision_driver_user catches all exceptions silently and writes warning into provisioning_error; admin gets no surfaced error in the create response.
test_credentials: Admin surya.yadavalli@nylaairwater.earth/test123 worked. Driver TEST UI Drv 043422 with phone 7704342288 created during this run (will be cleaned up by pytest fixture; the UI-created driver remains).
seed_data_creation: Created one TEST UI driver via UI (TEST UI Drv 043422, phone 7704342288) and several short-lived pytest drivers (deleted by fixture).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Driver backend + login UI + admin credentials modal verified. Next steps if anyone wants deeper coverage: (1) seed a distributor schedule, approve+start it, push a /driver/tracking/ping, then load DeliveryScheduleDetail as distributor and assert LiveDriverMap renders marker; (2) verify Login.js role-based redirect for Driver users via the shared /login route.
